name: PULMONIC (PULMONARY) STENOSIS

demographics.Frequency: pulmonary artery stenosis without VSD in 8% of all CHD

dominant_finding: poststenotic enlargement of main + left PA
distribution: increased convexity of anterior cardiac border on LAO
discriminating_features: systolic doming of pulmonary valve (= incomplete opening)
normal / diminished / increased pulmonary vascularity (depending on presence + nature of
enlarged pulmonary trunk + left pulmonary artery (= poststenotic dilatation)
prominent left pulmonary artery + normal right pulmonary a.
hypertrophy of RV with reduced size of RV chamber:

general: systolic doming of pulmonary valve (= incomplete opening)
normal / diminished / increased pulmonary vascularity (depending on presence + nature of
enlarged pulmonary trunk + left pulmonary artery (= poststenotic dilatation)
prominent left pulmonary artery + normal right pulmonary a.
hypertrophy of RV with reduced size of RV chamber:
elevation of cardiac apex
increased convexity of anterior cardiac border on LAO
diminution of retrosternal clear space
cor pulmonale
mild enlargement of LA (reason unknown)
calcification of pulmonary valves in older adults (rare)
late-peaking jet
no dilatation of PA because of dissipation of RV force through elongated area of obstruction
dome-shaped valve:
mobile valve with 2-4 raphes
incomplete separation of valve cusps
jet of contrast through small central orifice
dilated main + left pulmonary artery
cauliflower-like thickening at free margin of leaflets
NO poststenotic dilatation
CXR: enlargement of main + left pulmonary artery
CT: poststenotic enlargement of main + left PA
normal size of right PA (not exposed to turbulent jet from stenotic valve due to 90° angle
bowing of interventricular septum to the left
decreased mobility of pliable + thin valve leaflets
ANGIO: increase in trabecular pattern of RV

clinical.Prognosis: death at mean age of 21 years if untreated
clinical.Rx: balloon valvuloplasty (for peak instantaneous gradient above 50 mmHg)
